title: santolina cupressiformis
intro: Santolina cupressiformis, also known as Cotton Lavender or Gray Santolina, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The medicinal properties of Santolina cupressiformis are said to offer health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
